log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a 69KB PE32 executable packed with ASPack, compiled with Borland Delphi. It has runtime modules and an overlay. Now I need to get the IOC types to understand what data is available for a

The IOC summary shows no network activity, no registry persistence keys, 1 dropped file, and 5 processes created. The earlier overview showed mutexes (ShimCacheMutex, itismutexone) and process activit

No network activity was detected. The malware appears to be a local-only dropper/payload. I need to investigate the registry changes to check for persistence mechanisms, as the IOC summary showed 0 pe

No registry persistence keys were found, which is interesting given the 'at.exe' commands in the initial overview. The 'at.exe' commands might be a false positive or the persistence mechanism wasn't c

The malware drops one file with a hash that matches the sample itself (0415bdd23a878f92...). This suggests it might be unpacking itself or dropping a copy. I need to investigate the process activity t
The process activity confirms the execution chain: the malware spawns ntvdm.exe (Windows 95/98 compatibility layer) and at.exe (Task Scheduler). The 'at.exe' commands are used to schedule the malware
The CAPA behaviors confirm the malware is packed with ASPack (anti-analysis technique) and contains an embedded PE file (Execution::Install Additional Program [B0023]). The modulo operation and loops
